Par défaut, le code est en couleur sur VS Code, notamment pour les éléments comme [scan_local_files.py]().  Mais dès que j'essaie d'incrémenter, de faire Tab pour les caler comme je le souhaite sur le fichier, ça désactive la couleur et je perds la fonctionnalité. Pourquoi ?

C’est normal, et ce n’est pas de ta faute.
Ce comportement vient du moteur Markdown de VS Code.
🎯 Pourquoi tu perds la couleur ?
Parce que VS Code ne colorise les liens Markdown que lorsqu’ils sont au niveau “bloc texte normal”.
Dès que tu ajoutes :


une indentation (Tab ou espaces)


un préfixe qui n’est pas un symbole reconnu (comme - ou *)


ou que tu te retrouves à l’intérieur d’un bloc considéré comme “code” ou “liste imbriquée”


👉 VS Code change le mode de coloration et n’interprète plus les liens comme des liens.
En clair :
scssCopier le code[scan_local_files.py]()

➡️ coloré
Mais :
scssCopier le code    [scan_local_files.py]()

➡️ considéré comme “code block” → plus de couleur, plus de lien.
🧠 Raison technique
Dans Markdown, une ligne indentée de 4 espaces ou plus = bloc de code.
Et un bloc de code ignore toutes les syntaxes Markdown, y compris les liens.
✔️ Solutions possibles
Solution 1 — Utiliser des listes plutôt que des indentations
Au lieu de mettre des Tabs, on organise ainsi :
scssCopier le code### get
- [scan_local_files.py]() Repère les fichiers…
- [superscan.py]() SuperTool…

➡️ Couleurs conservées.
Solution 2 — Indentation légère (3 espaces max)
Pas plus de 3 espaces avant le lien :
scssCopier le code   [scan_local_files.py]()

➡️ Reste interprété comme texte, pas code → couleurs OK.
Solution 3 — Réglage VS Code (moins fiable)
Désactiver la stricte interprétation Markdown :
Extensions → Markdown → décocher “Render Indented Code Blocks”.
Mais le rendu GitHub ou tout autre éditeur restera strict → déconseillé.

👉 La meilleure approche pour EUREKAI
Ne jamais utiliser de Tab pour organiser ta documentation Markdown.
Utilise titres, listes, et sous-listes.
Si tu veux, je peux réécrire tout ton plan avec une structure visuelle propre sans perdre les couleurs, ni les blocs, ni la hiérarchie.
Tu veux que je reformate tout ton BigPlan ?
Mais pourtant, j'ai la même chose à deux lignes plus haut, et y'a pas de soucis. C'est un bug ?

Non, ce n’est pas un bug de VS Code.
C’est un piège très précis de Markdown, et c’est pour ça que ça te semble incohérent.
Voici exactement ce qui se passe 👇

✅ Pourquoi deux lignes identiques n’ont pas le même rendu ?
Même si visuellement elles te semblent pareilles, elles ne le sont pas pour le moteur Markdown.
🔍 La raison :
👉 Le Markdown se base sur le nombre exact d’espaces avant la ligne.


4 espaces = bloc de code → tous les liens deviennent du texte brut → pas de couleur


3 espaces ou moins = texte normal → liens restent actifs


Et le vrai piège ?
⚠️ Un Tab n’est pas toujours compté comme 4 espaces.
Selon :


tes réglages VS Code (tabSize)


s’il y a des espaces mélangés avec des tabs


ce qui précède dans le fichier (certaines syntaxes “verrouillent” le mode)


➡️ Deux lignes qui paraissent identiques peuvent en réalité avoir :


une indentation de 3 espaces


l’autre de 4 espaces
→ et boum, comportement complètement différent.


C’est pour ça que tu vois une ligne colorée, et la même juste en dessous non colorée.

🧪 Comment vérifier ?
Place ton curseur devant le [ de la ligne qui bug et fais :
👉 Cmd + I (Inspecteur Markdown)
VS Code va t’afficher :


soit “Paragraph” → rendu normal → couleurs OK


soit “Code Block” → rendu brut → couleurs KO


Tu vas voir immédiatement la différence.

🛠️ Comment le corriger ?
Tu as 3 options simples :
✔️ Option 1 — Réduire l’indentation
Passe de 4 espaces → 3 espaces maximum
scssCopier le code   [scan_local_files.py]()

✔️ Option 2 — Utiliser un tiret
scssCopier le code- [scan_local_files.py]()

✔️ Option 3 — Forcer VS Code à afficher les caractères invisibles
Menu : View → Render Whitespace → All
→ tu verras enfin clairement les espaces vs tabs.

🧨 Donc non : ce n’est pas un bug
C’est une interprétation Markdown stricte + l’effet sournois de la différence Tab/Spaces ou 3 espaces/4 espaces.
Si tu veux, je peux :


réécrire ton BigPlan avec une structure 100 % compatible Markdown,


ou te donner une règle simple d’organisation qui évite totalement ce problème.

✅ Explication précise à partir de ta capture VS Code

👉 Les lignes NON colorées sont indentées avec un TAB ou 4 espaces

En Markdown strict :
👉 Un titre suivi immédiatement d’un lien sans ligne vide = mode “paragraph continuation”
👉 S’il y a un retour en arrière (outline), VS Code peut basculer en mode inline code.

(ligne vide obligatoire)
lien

✅ 1 — Rappel de TES règles (les bonnes)
✔️ Chaque objet a :


ses méthodes centrales : Create / Read / Update / Delete / Execute / Engage


ses méthodes secondaires, dont scan() pour les objets “capteurs”


ses hooks : scan_hook_after, read_hook_after, etc.


✔️ Un SCÉNARIO n’est jamais une méthode d’objet
→ un scénario est un objet à part entière
→ avec des steps G / E / V / R
→ et des sous-méthodes qui appellent les méthodes des objets concernés.

directory


file


json_file


chat_file


PAS à un “library”.

→ ça ne correspond à aucune méthode centrale

Scénario ChatIntake (GEVR)

Appelle les méthodes secondaires scan() des objets concernés :

EXECUTE

chat.extract.* (sous-prompts)


json.normalize()

VALIDATE

export.index()

- chat_vector_db/

- chat_exports/  
  Exports globaux (JSON + index + métadatas) pour d’autres scénarios.

    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target")"
    cat > "$target" << 'EOF'
$CONTENT$
EOF
  fi
}

  else
    echo "CREATE test: $target"
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target")"
    cat > "$target" << 'EOF'
def test_placeholder():
    assert True
EOF
  fi
}

def convert_functions_format(catalog: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
    results = []
    for item in catalog:
        results.append(
            {
                "source_path": item.get("path"),
                "object": None,
                "central_method": None,
                "tags": [],
                "status": "candidate",
            }
        )
    return results
EOF

def tag_functions(functions: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
    for f in functions:
        if not f.get("tags"):
            f["tags"] = []
        if "method" not in f.get("tags", []):
            f["tags"].append("method")
    return functions
EOF
fi

import csv
from pathlib import Path

def export_functions_index(functions: list[dict], index_path: str) -> str:
    path = Path(index_path)
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    fieldnames = ["source_path", "object", "central_method", "status"]
    with path.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames, delimiter=";")
        writer.writeheader()
        for fn in functions:
            writer.writerow(
                {
                    "source_path": fn.get("source_path", ""),
                    "object": fn.get("object", ""),
                    "central_method": fn.get("central_method", ""),
                    "status": fn.get("status", ""),
                }
            )
    return str(path)
EOF
fi

  cat > "$target" << 'EOF'
from pathlib import Path
import json

def prepare_function_vectors(functions: list[dict], output_dir: str) -> str:
    out = Path(output_dir) / "function_vectors.jsonl"
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with out.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        for fn in functions:
            text = fn.get("source_path", "")
            record = {"text": text, "meta": fn}
            f.write(json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
    return str(out)

def prepare_chat_vectors(chats: list[dict], output_dir: str) -> str:
    out = Path(output_dir) / "chat_vector_ready.jsonl"
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with out.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        for chat in chats:
            record = {"text": chat.get("text", ""), "meta": chat}
            f.write(json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
    return str(out)
EOF
fi

import csv
from pathlib import Path
import json

def build_chat_index_from_raw(raw_dir: str, index_path: str) -> str:
    raw = Path(raw_dir)
    out = Path(index_path)
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    fieldnames = ["file_path", "model", "provider"]
    with out.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames, delimiter=";")
        writer.writeheader()
        for p in raw.rglob("*.json"):
            try:
                data = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            except Exception:
                data = {}
            writer.writerow(
                {
                    "file_path": str(p),
                    "model": data.get("model", ""),
                    "provider": data.get("provider", ""),
                }
            )
    return str(out)
EOF
fi

from pathlib import Path
import json

def chat_to_json(source_path: str, output_path: str) -> str:
    src = Path(source_path)
    out = Path(output_path)
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    data = json.loads(src.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    out.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    return str(out)
EOF
fi

from pathlib import Path

def process_chats_with_ia(index_path: str) -> list[dict]:
    path = Path(index_path)
    lines = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
    if not lines:
        return []
    header = lines[0].split(";")
    results = []
    for row in lines[1:]:
        parts = row.split(";")
        record = dict(zip(header, parts))
        record["topics"] = []
        record["outputs"] = []
        results.append(record)
    return results
EOF
fi

         "$TEST_FUNC_DIR" "$TEST_CHAT_DIR" \
         "$GLOBAL_OUTPUTS/FUNCTIONS" "$GLOBAL_OUTPUTS/CHATS"


write_py() {
  local target="$1"
  shift
  if [ -e "$target" ]; then
    echo "SKIP (exists): $target"
  else
    echo "CREATE: $target"
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target")"
    cat > "$target" << 'EOF'
'"$@"'
EOF
  fi
}

write_test() {
  local target="$1"
  if [ -e "$target" ]; then
    echo "SKIP test (exists): $target"
  else
    echo "CREATE test: $target"
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target")"
    cat > "$target" << 'EOF'
def test_placeholder():
    assert True
EOF
  fi
}

# superscan.py
write_py "$FUNC_DIR/superscan.py" \
'from pathlib import Path
from typing import Iterable


def superscan(items: Iterable[Path]) -> list[dict]:
    """
    SuperTool agnostique : prend une liste de Paths,
    renvoie un catalogue brut d\'éléments.
    """
    catalog: list[dict] = []
    for p in items:
        catalog.append({"path": str(p)})
    return catalog'

# convert_functions_format.py
write_py "$FUNC_DIR/convert_functions_format.py" \
'from typing import List, Dict


def convert_functions_format(catalog: List[Dict]) -> List[Dict]:
    """
    Convertit le catalogue brut en objets normalisés EUREKAI.
    Ne fait pas encore d\'analyse de code, seulement la structure.
    """
    results: List[Dict] = []
    for item in catalog:
        results.append(
            {
                "source_path": item.get("path"),
                "object": None,
                "central_method": None,
                "tags": [],
                "status": "candidate",
            }
        )
    return results'

def tag_functions(functions: List[Dict]) -> List[Dict]:
    """
    Ajoute des tags de base aux fonctions (squelette).
    À enrichir plus tard avec l\'IA et la logique CRUDAE.
    """
    for f in functions:
        tags = f.get("tags") or []
        if "method" not in tags:
            tags.append("method")
        f["tags"] = tags
    return functions'

    Retourne une liste d\'erreurs (vide si tout va bien).

    errors: List[Dict] = []

from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Dict


def export_functions_index(functions: List[Dict], index_path: str) -> str:
    """
    Exporte un index CSV des fonctions (chemin, objet, méthode centrale, statut).
    """
    path = Path(index_path)
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    fieldnames = ["source_path", "object", "central_method", "status"]
    with path.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames, delimiter=";")
        writer.writeheader()
        for fn in functions:
            writer.writerow(
                {
                    "source_path": fn.get("source_path", ""),
                    "object": fn.get("object", ""),
                    "central_method": fn.get("central_method", ""),
                    "status": fn.get("status", ""),
                }
            )
    return str(path)'

write_py "$COMMON_FUNC_DIR/prepare_vector_data.py" \
'from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Dict
import json


def prepare_function_vectors(functions: List[Dict], output_dir: str) -> str:
    """
    Prépare un fichier JSONL pour vectorisation des fonctions.
    """
    out = Path(output_dir) / "function_vectors.jsonl"
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with out.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        for fn in functions:
            text = fn.get("source_path", "")
            record = {"text": text, "meta": fn}
            f.write(json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
    return str(out)


def prepare_chat_vectors(chats: List[Dict], output_dir: str) -> str:
    """
    Prépare un fichier JSONL pour vectorisation des chats.
    """
    out = Path(output_dir) / "chat_vector_ready.jsonl"
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with out.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        for chat in chats:
            record = {"text": chat.get("text", ""), "meta": chat}
            f.write(json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
    return str(out)'

# build_chat_index_from_raw.py
write_py "$CHAT_DIR/build_chat_index_from_raw.py" \
'import csv
from pathlib import Path
import json


def build_chat_index_from_raw(raw_dir: str, index_path: str) -> str:
    """
    Construit MASTER_CHAT_INDEX.csv à partir des fichiers JSON bruts.
    """
    raw = Path(raw_dir)
    out = Path(index_path)
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    fieldnames = ["file_path", "model", "provider"]
    with out.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames, delimiter=";")
        writer.writeheader()
        for p in raw.rglob("*.json"):
            try:
                data = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            except Exception:
                data = {}
            writer.writerow(
                {
                    "file_path": str(p),
                    "model": data.get("model", ""),
                    "provider": data.get("provider", ""),
                }
            )
    return str(out)'

# chat_to_json.py
write_py "$CHAT_DIR/chat_to_json.py" \
'from pathlib import Path
import json


def chat_to_json(source_path: str, output_path: str) -> str:
    """
    Normalise un fichier de chat vers un JSON propriétaire (pour plus tard).
    """
    src = Path(source_path)
    out = Path(output_path)
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    data = json.loads(src.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    out.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    return str(out)'

# process_chats_with_ia.py (squelette)
write_py "$CHAT_DIR/process_chats_with_ia.py" \
'from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Dict


def process_chats_with_ia(index_path: str) -> List[Dict]:
    """
    Squelette : lit un index CSV et enrichit les lignes avec placeholders
    pour topics / outputs.
    """
    path = Path(index_path)
    if not path.exists():
        return []
    lines = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
    if not lines:
        return []
    header = lines[0].split(";")
    results: List[Dict] = []
    for row in lines[1:]:
        parts = row.split(";")
        record = dict(zip(header, parts))
        record["topics"] = []
        record["outputs"] = []
        results.append(record)
    return results'

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Iterable, List, Dict


def superscan(items: Iterable[Path]) -> List[Dict]:
    """
    SuperTool agnostique : prend une liste de Paths,
    renvoie un catalogue brut d'éléments.
    """
    catalog: List[Dict] = []
    for p in items:
        catalog.append({"path": str(p)})
    return catalog

from typing import List, Dict


def convert_functions_format(catalog: List[Dict]) -> List[Dict]:
    """
    Convertit le catalogue brut en objets normalisés EUREKAI.
    Ne fait pas encore d'analyse de code, seulement la structure.
    """
    results: List[Dict] = []
    for item in catalog:
        results.append(
            {
                "source_path": item.get("path"),
                "object": None,
                "central_method": None,
                "tags": [],
                "status": "candidate",
            }
        )
    return results

from typing import List, Dict


def tag_functions(functions: List[Dict]) -> List[Dict]:
    """
    Ajoute des tags de base aux fonctions (squelette).
    À enrichir plus tard avec l'IA et la logique CRUDAE.

        f["tags"] = tags

import csv
from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Dict


def export_functions_index(functions: List[Dict], index_path: str) -> str:
    """
    Exporte un index CSV des fonctions (chemin, objet, méthode centrale, statut).
    """
    path = Path(index_path)
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    fieldnames = ["source_path", "object", "central_method", "status"]
    with path.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames, delimiter=";")
        writer.writeheader()
        for fn in functions:
            writer.writerow(
                {
                    "source_path": fn.get("source_path", ""),
                    "object": fn.get("object", ""),
                    "central_method": fn.get("central_method", ""),
                    "status": fn.get("status", ""),
                }
            )
    return str(path)
EOF

bashCopier le codecat > code/RESOURCES/FUNCTIONS/prepare_vector_data.py << 'EOF'
from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Dict
import json


def prepare_function_vectors(functions: List[Dict], output_dir: str) -> str:
    """
    Prépare un fichier JSONL pour vectorisation des fonctions.
    """
    out = Path(output_dir) / "function_vectors.jsonl"
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with out.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        for fn in functions:
            text = fn.get("source_path", "")
            record = {"text": text, "meta": fn}
            f.write(json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
    return str(out)


def prepare_chat_vectors(chats: List[Dict], output_dir: str) -> str:
    """
    Prépare un fichier JSONL pour vectorisation des chats.
    """
    out = Path(output_dir) / "chat_vector_ready.jsonl"
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with out.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        for chat in chats:
            record = {"text": chat.get("text", ""), "meta": chat}
            f.write(json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
    return str(out)

import csv
from pathlib import Path
import json


def build_chat_index_from_raw(raw_dir: str, index_path: str) -> str:
    """
    Construit MASTER_CHAT_INDEX.csv à partir des fichiers JSON bruts.
    """
    raw = Path(raw_dir)
    out = Path(index_path)
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    fieldnames = ["file_path", "model", "provider"]
    with out.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=fieldnames, delimiter=";")
        writer.writeheader()
        for p in raw.rglob("*.json"):
            try:
                data = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            except Exception:
                data = {}
            writer.writerow(
                {
                    "file_path": str(p),
                    "model": data.get("model", ""),
                    "provider": data.get("provider", ""),
                }
            )
    return str(out)
EOF

from pathlib import Path

def chat_to_json(source_path: str, output_path: str) -> str:
    """
    Normalise un fichier de chat vers un JSON propriétaire (pour plus tard).
    """
    src = Path(source_path)
    out = Path(output_path)

    data = json.loads(src.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))

from pathlib import Path
from typing import List, Dict


def process_chats_with_ia(index_path: str) -> List[Dict]:
    """
    Squelette : lit un index CSV et enrichit les lignes avec placeholders
    pour topics / outputs.
    """
    path = Path(index_path)
    if not path.exists():
        return []
    lines = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
    if not lines:
        return []
    header = lines[0].split(";")
    results: List[Dict] = []
    for row in lines[1:]:
        parts = row.split(";")
        record = dict(zip(header, parts))
        record["topics"] = []
        record["outputs"] = []
        results.append(record)
    return results
EOF

import importlib.util
import pathlib
import traceback
from datetime import datetime

GLOBAL_ROOT = pathlib.Path("/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO")
PROJECT_NAME = "EURKAI"
TESTS_BASE = GLOBAL_ROOT / "_TESTS" / PROJECT_NAME


def import_and_run(test_file: pathlib.Path):
    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(test_file.stem, test_file)
    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    assert spec.loader is not None
    spec.loader.exec_module(module)  # type: ignore
    for name in dir(module):
        if name.startswith("test_"):
            func = getattr(module, name)
            if callable(func):
                func()


def main():
    results = []
    for test_file in TESTS_BASE.rglob("test_*.py"):
        rel = test_file.relative_to(TESTS_BASE)
        parts = rel.parts
        if len(parts) < 4:
            # EURKAI/<chantier>/<function>/test_vX.py
            continue
        project, chantier, function_name, *_ = parts
        ok = True
        error = None
        try:
            import_and_run(test_file)
        except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
            ok = False
            error = f"{e}\n{traceback.format_exc()}"
        results.append((project, chantier, function_name, test_file, ok, error))

    for project, chantier, function_name, test_file, ok, error in results:
        report_dir = test_file.parent
        report_path = report_dir / "validation_report.md"
        ts = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        status = "OK" if ok else "FAIL"
        content_lines = [
            f"# Validation report — {project}/{chantier}/{function_name}",
            "",
            f"- datetime: {ts} UTC",
            f"- status: {status}",
            f"- test_file: {test_file}",
            "",
        ]
        if error:
            content_lines.append("## Error")
            content_lines.append("")
            content_lines.append("```")
            content_lines.append(error)
            content_lines.append("```")
        report_path.write_text("\n".join(content_lines), encoding="utf-8")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
EOF

"""
Orchestrator for the FunctionLibrary_Intake scenario (GEVR).
"""

from typing import Any, Dict, List

from .function_intake_get import function_intake_get
from .function_intake_execute import function_intake_execute
from .function_intake_validate import function_intake_validate
from .function_intake_render import function_intake_render


def function_library_intake(
    root_paths: List[str],
    options: Dict[str, Any] | None = None,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Run the full FunctionLibrary_Intake scenario:
    - GET: scan local files, build raw catalog
    - EXECUTE: convert and tag functions
    - VALIDATE: run schema + index checks
    - RENDER: prepare final payload
    """
    if options is None:
        options = {}

    context: Dict[str, Any] = {"options": options}

    context = function_intake_get(context, root_paths)
    context = function_intake_execute(context)
    context = function_intake_validate(context)
    context = function_intake_render(context)

    return context

bashCopier le codecd "/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_INPUTS/EURKAI/METHODLIBRARY" && cat > EURKAI_METHODLIBRARY/code/RESOURCES/FUNCTIONS/function_library_intake/scan_local_files.py << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env python3

"""
Scan local directories to find candidate Python function files.
"""

import os
from typing import List


def scan_local_files(root_paths: List[str]) -> List[str]:
    """
    Return a list of absolute paths to .py files under the given roots.
    """
    results: List[str] = []

    for root in root_paths:
        if not os.path.isdir(root):
            continue

        for dirpath, _, filenames in os.walk(root):
            for name in filenames:
                if name.endswith(".py"):
                    full_path = os.path.join(dirpath, name)
                    results.append(os.path.abspath(full_path))

    return results
EOF

#!/usr/bin/env python3

"""
SuperScan: normalize a list of raw paths into a simple catalog structure.
"""

import os
from typing import Dict, List


def superscan(file_paths: List[str]) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
    """
    Turn a list of file paths into a small catalog:
      - path
      - file_name
      - dir_name
    """
    catalog: List[Dict[str, str]] = []

    for path in file_paths:
        file_name = os.path.basename(path)
        dir_name = os.path.dirname(path)
        catalog.append(
            {
                "path": path,
                "file_name": file_name,
                "dir_name": dir_name,
            }
        )

    return catalog
EOF

6️⃣ convert_functions_format.py — conversion en “JSON logique”
bashCopier le codecd "/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_INPUTS/EURKAI/METHODLIBRARY" && cat > EURKAI_METHODLIBRARY/code/RESOURCES/FUNCTIONS/function_library_intake/convert_functions_format.py << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env python3

"""
Convert raw catalog entries into internal function records.
"""

import os
from typing import Dict, List


def convert_functions_format(
    catalog: List[Dict[str, str]],
) -> List[Dict[str, str]]:
    """
    Basic conversion:
      - id: file stem
      - name: file stem
      - path: absolute path
    """
    records: List[Dict[str, str]] = []

    for entry in catalog:
        path = entry["path"]
        stem, _ = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(path))
        record = {
            "id": stem,
            "name": stem,
            "path": path,
        }
        records.append(record)

    return records
EOF

bashCopier le codecd "/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_INPUTS/EURKAI/METHODLIBRARY" && cat > EURKAI_METHODLIBRARY/code/RESOURCES/FUNCTIONS/function_library_intake/validate_functions_schema.py << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env python3

"""
Check presence of required fields on function records.
"""

from typing import Dict, List, Tuple


REQUIRED_FIELDS = ["id", "name", "path"]


def validate_functions_schema(
    records: List[Dict[str, str]],
) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
    """
    Return (ok, errors). Errors are textual messages.
    """
    errors: List[str] = []

    for idx, record in enumerate(records):
        for field in REQUIRED_FIELDS:
            if field not in record or record[field] in (None, ""):
                errors.append(f"Record {idx}: missing field '{field}'")

    return len(errors) == 0, errors
EOF

bashCopier le codecd "/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_INPUTS/EURKAI/METHODLIBRARY" && cat > EURKAI_METHODLIBRARY/code/RESOURCES/FUNCTIONS/function_library_intake/validate_functions_index.py << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env python3

"""
Check basic index consistency for function records.
"""

from typing import Dict, List, Tuple


def validate_functions_index(
    records: List[Dict[str, str]],
) -> Tuple[bool, List[str]]:
    """
    Minimal check:
      - no duplicate ids
      - no duplicate (name, path) pairs
    """
    errors: List[str] = []
    seen_ids = set()
    seen_name_path = set()

    for idx, record in enumerate(records):
        rid = record.get("id")
        key = (record.get("name"), record.get("path"))

        if rid in seen_ids:
            errors.append(f"Record {idx}: duplicate id '{rid}'")
        else:
            seen_ids.add(rid)

        if key in seen_name_path:
            errors.append(f"Record {idx}: duplicate (name, path) {key}")
        else:
            seen_name_path.add(key)

    return len(errors) == 0, errors
EOF

#!/usr/bin/env python3

"""
Build a simple index view for function records.
"""

from typing import Dict, List, Any


def export_functions_index(
    records: List[Dict[str, str]],
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """
    Returns a minimal index:
      - by_id: dict id -> record
      - all_ids: list of ids
    """
    by_id: Dict[str, Dict[str, str]] = {}
    for r in records:
        rid = r.get("id")
        if not rid:
            continue
        by_id[rid] = r

    index = {
        "by_id": by_id,
        "all_ids": list(by_id.keys()),
    }
    return index
EOF

create "scan_local_files.py" '
import os

def scan_local_files(directory):
    results = []
    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(directory):
        for f in files:
            if f.endswith(".py"):
                results.append(os.path.join(root, f))
    return {"files": results}
'

create "superscan.py" '
import json
import os

def superscan(path):
    if os.path.isdir(path):
        items = os.listdir(path)
        return {"type": "directory", "items": items}
    if os.path.isfile(path):
        with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            content = f.read()
        return {"type": "file", "content": content}
    return {"error": "not_found"}
'

create "function_intake_get.py" '
from superscan import superscan

def function_intake_get(target):
    return superscan(target)
'

create "convert_functions_format.py" '
def convert_functions_format(data):
    converted = []
    for f in data.get("files", []):
        converted.append({"path": f, "name": f.split("/")[-1]})
    return {"converted": converted}
'

create "prepare_vector_data.py" '
def prepare_vector_data(functions_list):
    vectors = []
    for f in functions_list.get("converted", []):
        vectors.append({"id": f["name"], "text": f["name"]})
    return {"vectors": vectors}
'

create "tag_functions.py" '
def tag_functions(converted):
    tagged = []
    for c in converted.get("converted", []):
        if "validate" in c["name"]:
            tag = "validator"
        elif "render" in c["name"]:
            tag = "renderer"
        elif "execute" in c["name"]:
            tag = "executor"
        else:
            tag = "generic"
        tagged.append({"name": c["name"], "tag": tag})
    return {"tagged": tagged}
'

create "function_intake_validate.py" '
def function_intake_validate(data):
    if "files" not in data:
        return {"valid": False, "error": "missing_files"}
    if not data["files"]:
        return {"valid": False, "error": "empty"}
    return {"valid": True}
'

create "function_intake_execute.py" '
from function_intake_get import function_intake_get
from convert_functions_format import convert_functions_format
from prepare_vector_data import prepare_vector_data
from tag_functions import tag_functions
from function_intake_validate import function_intake_validate

def function_intake_execute(directory):
    raw = function_intake_get(directory)
    validation = function_intake_validate(raw)
    if not validation["valid"]:
        return {"error": validation["error"], "stage": "validate"}

    converted = convert_functions_format(raw)
    vectors = prepare_vector_data(converted)
    tags = tag_functions(converted)

    return {
        "raw": raw,
        "converted": converted,
        "vectors": vectors,
        "tags": tags
    }
'

👉 Un shebang ne doit JAMAIS être tapé dans le terminal.
Il doit être à l’intérieur d’un fichier.

#!/bin/zsh

BASE_DIR="/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_INPUTS/EURKAI/CHATLIBRARY/chat_library_intake"

mkdir -p "$BASE_DIR"

create_file() {
  filepath="$BASE_DIR/$1"
  content="$2"
  echo "$content" > "$filepath"
}

create_file "scan_chats.py" "def scan_chats(path):\n    return {'scanned': path}"
create_file "parse_chat_json.py" "def parse_chat_json(raw):\n    return {'parsed': raw}"
create_file "build_chat_index.py" "def build_chat_index(data):\n    return {'index': True}"
create_file "export_chat_vectors.py" "def export_chat_vectors(entries):\n    return {'vectors': len(entries)}"
create_file "merge_sessions.py" "def merge_sessions(sessions):\n    return {'merged': len(sessions)}"
create_file "prepare_training_dataset.py" "def prepare_training_dataset(data):\n    return {'dataset': True}"
create_file "chat_intake_get.py" "def chat_intake_get():\n    return {'status':'ok'}"
create_file "chat_intake_execute.py" "def chat_intake_execute():\n    return {'executed':True}"
create_file "chat_intake_validate.py" "def chat_intake_validate():\n    return {'validated':True}"
create_file "chat_intake_render.py" "def chat_intake_render():\n    return {'render':'done'}"

def chat_intake_execute(config):
    """
    Simule l’exécution de l’intake à partir d’une config.
    """
    if not isinstance(config, dict):
        return {"status": "error", "error": "invalid_config_type"}

    required = ["source_root", "output_root", "index_files"]
    missing = [k for k in required if k not in config]
    if missing:
        return {"status": "error", "error": "missing_keys", "missing": missing}

    return {
        "status": "ok",
        "processed": True,
        "source_root": config["source_root"],
        "output_root": config["output_root"],
    }
PYEOF

cat > chat_intake_validate.py << 'PYEOF'
def chat_intake_validate(result):
    """
    Valide le résultat de chat_intake_execute.
    """
    if not isinstance(result, dict):
        return {"status": "error", "error": "invalid_result_type"}

    if result.get("status") != "ok":
        return {"status": "error", "error": "execute_failed"}

    if "processed" not in result:
        return {"status": "error", "error": "missing_processed_flag"}

    return {"status": "ok", "valid": True}
PYEOF

cat > chat_intake_render.py << 'PYEOF'
def chat_intake_render(result):
    """
    Retourne une petite représentation textuelle du résultat.
    """
    status = result.get("status", "unknown")
    processed = result.get("processed", False)
    return f"chat_intake(status={status}, processed={processed})"
PYEOF

import os
import traceback
from datetime import datetime

def load_function(path, func):
    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(func, path)
    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
    return getattr(mod, func)

def run_test():
    this_dir = os.path.dirname(__file__)
    func_name = os.path.basename(this_dir)  # folder = function_name
    project = "EURKAI"
    chantier = os.path.basename(os.path.dirname(this_dir))

    function_path = f"/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_INPUTS/{project}/{chantier}/{func_name}.py"

    report_path = os.path.join(this_dir, "validation_report.md")

    try:
        f = load_function(function_path, func_name)
        result = f()

        with open(report_path, "w") as r:
            r.write(f"# Validation Report — {func_name}\n")
            r.write(f"**Date** : {datetime.now()}\n")
            r.write(f"**Status** : PASSED\n")
            r.write(f"**Output** : {result}\n")

        print(f"[OK] {func_name}")
    except Exception as e:
        with open(report_path, "w") as r:
            r.write(f"# Validation Report — {func_name}\n")
            r.write(f"**Date** : {datetime.now()}\n")
            r.write(f"**Status** : FAILED\n")
            r.write(f"**Error** :\n```\n{traceback.format_exc()}\n```")
        print(f"[FAIL] {func_name}")
        raise

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run_test()

pythonCopier le codeimport importlib.util
import os
import traceback
from datetime import datetime

def load_function(file_path, function_name):
    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(function_name, file_path)
    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
    return getattr(mod, function_name)

def run_test():
    # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    # Déduction automatique des chemins
    # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    this_test_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))     # dossier du test
    func_name = os.path.basename(this_test_dir)                    # nom du répertoire = nom de la fonction
    chantier = os.path.basename(os.path.dirname(this_test_dir))    # parent = chantier
    project = os.path.basename(                                     # parent au-dessus
        os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(this_test_dir))
    )

    # Chemin réel vers la fonction testée
    function_path = f"/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_INPUTS/{project}/{chantier}/{func_name}.py"

    # Chemin du rapport
    validation_report = os.path.join(this_test_dir, "validation_report.md")

    # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    # Exécution du test
    # ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    try:
        f = load_function(function_path, func_name)
        result = f()

        with open(validation_report, "w") as r:
            r.write(f"# Validation Report — {func_name}\n")
            r.write(f"**Date :** {datetime.now()}\n")
            r.write(f"**Status :** PASSED\n")
            r.write("**Output :**\n")
            r.write("```\n")
            r.write(str(result))
            r.write("\n```\n")

        print(f"[OK] {func_name}")

    except Exception as e:
        with open(validation_report, "w") as r:
            r.write(f"# Validation Report — {func_name}\n")
            r.write(f"**Date :** {datetime.now()}\n")
            r.write("**Status :** FAILED\n")
            r.write("**Error :**\n")
            r.write("```\n")
            r.write(traceback.format_exc())
            r.write("\n```\n")

        print(f"[FAIL] {func_name}")
        raise

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run_test()

import importlib.util
from datetime import datetime

# -----------------------------------------------------
# 1. Détection automatique du contexte
# -----------------------------------------------------

TEST_FILE = os.path.abspath(__file__)
TEST_NAME = os.path.basename(TEST_FILE)                    # ex: test_scan_local_files.py
FUNCTION_NAME = TEST_NAME.replace("test_", "").replace(".py", "")  # ex: scan_local_files
PROJECT_NAME = "EURKAI"
CHANTIER_NAME = "CHATLIBRARY"  # ou METHODLIBRARY selon dossier ; détecté ci-dessous

# Le chantier est déduit automatiquement depuis le chemin
split_path = TEST_FILE.split(os.sep)
if "METHODLIBRARY" in split_path:
    CHANTIER_NAME = "METHODLIBRARY"
elif "CHATLIBRARY" in split_path:
    CHANTIER_NAME = "CHATLIBRARY"

# -----------------------------------------------------
# 2. Chemin de la fonction source à tester
# -----------------------------------------------------
FUNCTION_PATH = os.path.join(
    "/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_INPUTS",
    PROJECT_NAME,
    CHANTIER_NAME,
    FUNCTION_NAME,
    FUNCTION_NAME + ".py"
)

# -----------------------------------------------------
# 3. Import dynamique du module
# -----------------------------------------------------
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(FUNCTION_NAME, FUNCTION_PATH)
module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(module)

# La fonction doit exister dans le module
tested_function = getattr(module, FUNCTION_NAME)

# -----------------------------------------------------
# 4. Exécution du test
# -----------------------------------------------------
success = False
error_message = ""

try:
    result = tested_function()
    success = True
except Exception as e:
    success = False
    error_message = str(e)

# -----------------------------------------------------
# 5. Génération du rapport de validation
# -----------------------------------------------------

REPORT_DIR = os.path.join(
    "/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_TESTS",
    PROJECT_NAME,
    CHANTIER_NAME,
    FUNCTION_NAME
)

os.makedirs(REPORT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

REPORT_FILE = os.path.join(REPORT_DIR, "validation_report.md")

timestamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

with open(REPORT_FILE, "w") as f:
    f.write(f"# Validation Report — {FUNCTION_NAME}\n")
    f.write(f"**Project:** {PROJECT_NAME}\n\n")
    f.write(f"**Chantier:** {CHANTIER_NAME}\n\n")
    f.write(f"**Function tested:** `{FUNCTION_NAME}`\n\n")
    f.write(f"**Test file:** `{TEST_NAME}`\n\n")
    f.write(f"**Execution time:** {timestamp}\n\n")

    if success:
        f.write("## ✅ Result: SUCCESS\n\n")
        f.write(f"Returned value:\n```\n{result}\n```\n")
    else:
        f.write("## ❌ Result: FAILURE\n\n")
        f.write(f"### Error:\n```\n{error_message}\n```\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------
# 6. Sortie console minimale
# -----------------------------------------------------
if success:
    print(f"[OK] {FUNCTION_NAME} — validation_report.md generated.")
else:
    print(f"[FAIL] {FUNCTION_NAME} — see validation_report.md for details.")

bashCopier le codecd /Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_INPUTS/EURKAI

cat > install_all_tests.sh << 'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -e

BASE_INPUT="/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_INPUTS/EURKAI"
BASE_TEST="/Users/nathalie/Dropbox/____BIG_BOFF___/PROJETS/PRO/_TESTS/EURKAI"

create_test_file() {
  local chantier="$1"
  local function_dir="$2"
  local module_basename="$3"
  local function_name="$4"

  local test_dir="$BASE_TEST/EURKAI/$chantier/$function_name"
  mkdir -p "$test_dir"
  local file="$test_dir/test_${module_basename}.py"
  local module_rel_path="$function_dir/$module_basename"

  cat > "$file" << PYEOF
import importlib.util
import os
import traceback

PROJECT_NAME = "EURKAI"
CHANTIER_NAME = "${chantier}"
FUNCTION_NAME = "${function_name}"
MODULE_REL_PATH = "${module_rel_path}"

BASE_INPUT = "${BASE_INPUT}"
MODULE_PATH = os.path.join(BASE_INPUT, PROJECT_NAME, CHANTIER_NAME, MODULE_REL_PATH + ".py")

def load_target():
    if not os.path.exists(MODULE_PATH):
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Target function file not found: {MODULE_PATH}")
    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(MODULE_REL_PATH.replace("/", "_"), MODULE_PATH)
    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
    return mod

def run_test():
    try:
        mod = load_target()
        target = getattr(mod, FUNCTION_NAME)
        result = target()
        status = "OK"
        details = f"Function returned: {result!r}"
    except Exception as e:
        status = "ERROR"
        details = "Exception during test:\\n" + "".join(
            traceback.format_exception(type(e), e, e.__traceback__)
        )

    report_dir = os.path.dirname(__file__)
    report_path = os.path.join(report_dir, "validation_report.md")

    lines = [
        f"# Validation Report — {FUNCTION_NAME}",
        "",
        f"- Project: {PROJECT_NAME}",
        f"- Chantier: {CHANTIER_NAME}",
        f"- Function: {FUNCTION_NAME}",
        f"- Module path: {MODULE_PATH}",
        f"- Status: {status}",
        "",
        "## Details",
        "",
        details,
        "",
    ]
    with open(report_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        f.write("\\n".join(lines))

    print(f"[{FUNCTION_NAME}] status={status}")
    print(details)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run_test()
PYEOF
}

# ========= METHODLIBRARY =========
# tous les modules sont dans function_library_intake/

create_test_file "METHODLIBRARY" "function_library_intake" "scan_local_files" "scan_local_files"
create_test_file "METHODLIBRARY" "function_library_intake" "superscan" "superscan"

# ingestion des chats

# index + vectorisation
